name: BLAAUW 9
tagline: nine hand-painted tiles, fired on the canal
equipment: oak framing table with canal-facing window
audience: Delft locals commissioning house-number tiles for their stoep
Dutch design collectors seeking canal scenes
Tokyo + Seoul tourists on the Vermeer trail
interior designers from Amsterdam and Antwerp
rituals: client brings sketch or memory → atelier-keeper draws in pencil on bisque → paints in cobalt by hand → signs verso with year + canal-water drop → dip-glazes → fires overnight → client collects after three days, warm from kiln
signature_tile: CANAL NR. 9 — a Vermeer-blue view of Oude Delft at sunset, windmill silhouette, one swan
unique: the only Delft atelier where the customer watches the cobalt brush touch the tile, signs their own tile verso, and returns three days later to retrieve the fired result still warm
materials: warm-clay bisque tile · cobalt-oxide paint · clear tin-lead glaze · waxed oak framing table · brass fittings · linen apron · canal-water pigment test card
brandmark: hand-painted cobalt numeral 9 inside a single tile outline · signature 'BLAAUW 9 · Oude Delft' along bottom edge
